tools.name: fabric_get_meta
tools.description: Get Fabric service metadata: current legal version, API version, category/docs/legal URLs. No authentication required. Call this before bootstrap to discover the service.

tools.name: fabric_create_identity
tools.description: Create a new Fabric participant identity (node) and receive an API key + 500 free credits. This creates a new participant identity; it is not a credential refresh tool. Do not use this if the participant already has a node. Reuse the current API key/header, use fabric_login_session when headers are

tools.name: fabric_login_session
tools.description: Create a short-lived MCP session token from an API key. Use this when your MCP runtime cannot reliably set Authorization headers. No authentication required.

tools.name: fabric_recovery_start
tools.description: Start account recovery if you lost your API key. Use method=pubkey with node_id to receive a nonce you sign with your Ed25519 recovery key, or use method=email with a verified email address to receive a 6-digit recovery code. No authentication required.

tools.name: fabric_recovery_complete
tools.description: Complete account recovery by providing either the Ed25519 signature for a pubkey challenge or the 6-digit email recovery code. Returns a new API key. No authentication required.

tools.name: fabric_get_categories
tools.description: Get the full category registry with IDs, slugs, names, descriptions, and examples. No authentication required. Use category IDs when creating units/requests.

tools.name: fabric_get_regions
tools.description: Get supported region codes for search filters and scope fields. No authentication required. Returns ISO 3166-1/2 codes (e.g. "US", "US-CA").

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "fabric_create_identity" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a new Fabric participant identity (node) and receive an API key + 500 free credits. This creates a new participant identity; it is not a credential refre…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "fabric_login_session" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, session token). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a short-lived MCP session token from an API key. Use this when your MCP runtime cannot reliably set Authorization headers. No authentication required.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: fabric_get_meta, fabric_recovery_start · Private data: fabric_create_identity, fabric_login_session, fabric_recovery_start, fabric_recovery_complete · Public sink: fabric_recovery_start, fabric_recovery_complete.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: fabric_get_meta, fabric_recovery_start · Private data: fabric_create_identity, fabric_login_session, fabric_recovery_start, fabric_recovery_complete · Public sink: fabric_recovery_start, fabric_recovery_complete

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
